rationale.R1.1: The stated thesis (instruction-following is the dominant bottleneck) diverges sharply from the conclusion's framing (quality crossover achieved, redirect effort to quality benchmarks); thesis-contribution coherence is a natural target of any 'check'.
rationale.R1.2: A material internal contradiction is present: the abstract opens by asserting instruction-following is the central unsolved problem, then concludes by claiming quality has crossed the production threshold and recommends investment in quality benchmarks — the conclusion undercuts the premise rather than following from it.
rationale.R1.3: The headline findings (BLEU gains, COMET-22 correlations, fluency ratings) are all translation-quality metrics, yet they are marshalled to support claims about instruction-adherence sufficiency and production readiness; the evidentiary warrant for the central claim is at risk.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus on internal logic and argument coherence — whether the thesis, findings, and conclusion are mutually consistent — or is this primarily a writing and proofreading pass for sentence-level quality?
